data.title: Portfolio Platform — Production Event-Driven Microservice Architecture
data.content:
Portfolio Platform — A Production-Grade Event-Driven Microservice Architecture

Live Site · GitHub (Frontend) · Admin Service · AI Platform · Notification Service · Analytics Platform

1. System Context & Motivation

This is not a typical portfolio site. It is a distributed system with five independently deployable services, connected via Apache Kafka event streaming, serving as both a personal site and a living engineering showcase. Every design decision addresses a real production concern: eventual consistency, exactly-once semantics, graceful degradation, observability, and cost efficiency.


┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                           SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE                                    │
│                                                                                  │
│  ┌──────────────┐         ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐           │
│  │   Visitors   │────────▶│  Portfolio Frontend (Next.js / Vercel)   │           │
│  └──────────────┘         └──────────────┬──────────────────────────┘           │
│                                          │                                       │
│          ┌───────────────────────────────┼───────────────────────────┐          │
│          │                               │                           │          │
│          ▼                               ▼                           ▼          │
│  ┌───────────────┐            ┌──────────────────┐         ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │  AI Platform  │            │  Admin Service    │         │   Analytics    │  │
│  │  (Agent+MCP)  │            │  (Content CRUD)   │         │   Platform     │  │
│  └───────┬───────┘            └────────┬─────────┘         └───────┬────────┘  │
│          │                             │                           │            │
│          │ MCP Tools                   │ Outbox→Kafka              │ Kafka      │
│          ▼                             ▼                           ▼            │
│  ┌───────────────┐      ┌──────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  │
│  │  Gemini/GPT   │      │         Apache Kafka          │  │  Valkey Cache  │  │
│  │  (LLM APIs)   │      │    (Aiven Managed Cluster)    │  │  (Dedup+Rate)  │  │
│  └───────────────┘      └──────┬──────────┬─────────────┘  └────────────────┘  │
│                                │          │                                     │
│                    ┌───────────┘          └──────────┐                          │
│                    ▼                                 ▼                          │
│          ┌──────────────────┐              ┌─────────────────────┐             │
│          │  Search Indexer  │              │ Notification Service │             │
│          │  + RAG Indexer   │              │  (Email Dispatch)    │             │
│          └────────┬─────────┘              └─────────────────────┘             │
│                   │                                                             │
│         ┌─────────┴──────────┐                                                 │
│         ▼                    ▼                                                  │
│  ┌─────────────┐    ┌──────────────┐                                           │
│  │ OpenSearch   │    │  PostgreSQL  │                                           │
│  │ (BM25+Syn)  │    │  + pgvector  │                                           │
│  └─────────────┘    └──────────────┘                                           │
│                                                                                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

2. Content Pipeline — Transactional Outbox with Kafka Fan-Out

The content pipeline is the backbone of the system. When content is created or updated, it must reliably propagate to search, RAG, and notification subsystems — without data loss and without distributed transactions.

Design Decision: Transactional Outbox pattern. The admin service writes both the content row and an outbox event in a single DB transaction. A post-commit hook publishes to Kafka only after the transaction succeeds — eliminating phantom events on rollback.


CONTENT PUBLISH FLOW
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Admin Dashboard                Admin Service                   Kafka
  ──────────────                ──────────────                  ─────
       │                              │                           │
       │  POST /api/admin/content     │                           │
       │─────────────────────────────▶│                           │
       │                              │                           │
       │                   ┌──────────┴──────────┐                │
       │                   │  BEGIN TRANSACTION  │                │
       │                   │                     │                │
       │                   │  1. INSERT Projects  │                │
       │                   │  2. INSERT version   │                │
       │                   │  3. INSERT outbox    │                │
       │                   │                     │                │
       │                   │  COMMIT             │                │
       │                   └──────────┬──────────┘                │
       │                              │                           │
       │                              │  @TransactionalEventListener(AFTER_COMMIT)
       │                              │───────────────────────────▶│
       │                              │                           │
       │                              │         Fan-out to 3 topics:
       │                              │         ├─ content.search.index.v1
       │                              │         ├─ content.rag.index.v1
       │                              │         └─ content.notification.feature-updates.v1
       │                              │                           │
       │  201 Created                 │                           │
       │◀─────────────────────────────│                           │

Why this matters:

  • Atomicity: Content row + outbox event in one transaction — no partial state.
  • At-least-once delivery: If Kafka publish fails, a recovery scheduler retries unflushed outbox rows.
  • Consumer independence: Search, RAG, and Notification consumers are fully decoupled — one can fail without affecting others.
  • Dead Letter Queue: Poison-pill events route to a DLQ topic for manual inspection.

3. Search Architecture — BM25 + Doc2Query + Synonym Expansion

The search system combines three techniques to maximize recall without runtime LLM costs:


SEARCH INDEXING PIPELINE (Index-Time)
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Kafka Topic                Search Indexer              Gemini API         OpenSearch
  ───────────               ───────────────            ──────────         ──────────
       │                          │                         │                  │
       │ content.search.index.v1  │                         │                  │
       │─────────────────────────▶│                         │                  │
       │                          │                         │                  │
       │                          │  generateContent()      │                  │
       │                          │  (title+summary+body)   │                  │
       │                          │────────────────────────▶│                  │
       │                          │                         │                  │
       │                          │  search_terms:          │                  │
       │                          │  "k8s, kubernetes,      │                  │
       │                          │   containers, docker,   │                  │
       │                          │   orchestration..."     │                  │
       │                          │◀────────────────────────│                  │
       │                          │                         │                  │
       │                          │  UPSERT document + search_terms            │
       │                          │───────────────────────────────────────────▶│
       │                          │                         │                  │


SEARCH QUERY FLOW (Query-Time, Zero LLM Cost)
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  User types: "k8s"
       │
       ▼
  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
  │  portfolio_search analyzer (query-time only):   │
  │                                                  │
  │  "k8s"                                          │
  │    → synonym_graph: "k8s" → "kubernetes"        │
  │    → english_stemmer: "kubernetes" → "kubernet"  │
  │                                                  │
  │  Matches indexed tokens from english analyzer    │
  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
       │
       ▼
  BM25 multi_match across:
    title^3, summary^2, content, search_terms^1.5
       │
       ▼
  Results: "Kubernetes vs Docker", "Gift Galaxy"

Three-Layer Recall Strategy:

  1. BM25 (baseline): Standard full-text search with English stemming on title, summary, content.
  2. Doc2Query Expansion (index-time): Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite generates 15-40 search terms per document — synonyms, related concepts, abbreviations, and natural-language question phrasings. Stored in search_terms field. Cost: ~/bin/zsh.01/1M input tokens, one-time per document.
  3. Synonym Graph (query-time): A synonym_graph filter expands abbreviations at search time (k8s→kubernetes, slc→salt lake city, js→javascript). Combined with English stemming. Zero cost — pure OpenSearch configuration.

Result: Users can search abbreviations, slang, related concepts, and partial phrases — all resolved via BM25 with zero runtime API calls.

4. AI Agent — Intent Classification + MCP Tool Orchestration

The chat widget ("Mr. Pot") is backed by a multi-model agent with strict tool governance:


AI AGENT REQUEST FLOW
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Chat Widget             Agent Service             MCP Gateway         Backend Services
  ───────────            ──────────────            ───────────         ────────────────
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │  SSE /api/agent/chat  │                        │                     │
       │──────────────────────▶│                        │                     │
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │            ┌──────────┴──────────┐             │                     │
       │            │ Intent Classification│             │                     │
       │            │ (Gemini 2.5 Flash)   │             │                     │
       │            │                      │             │                     │
       │            │ confidence < 0.7?    │             │                     │
       │            │ → escalate to GPT-4o │             │                     │
       │            └──────────┬──────────┘             │                     │
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │            ┌──────────┴──────────┐             │                     │
       │            │ RBAC Check:          │             │                     │
       │            │ JWT role → allowed   │             │                     │
       │            │ tools whitelist      │             │                     │
       │            └──────────┬──────────┘             │                     │
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │                       │  Tool invocation       │                     │
       │                       │  + idempotency key     │                     │
       │                       │───────────────────────▶│                     │
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │                       │              ┌─────────┴─────────┐           │
       │                       │              │ Schema validation  │           │
       │                       │              │ Risk gating        │           │
       │                       │              │ Dedup check        │           │
       │                       │              └─────────┬─────────┘           │
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │                       │                        │  Execute action     │
       │                       │                        │────────────────────▶│
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │                       │                        │  Result             │
       │                       │                        │◀────────────────────│
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │                       │  Tool result           │                     │
       │                       │◀───────────────────────│                     │
       │                       │                        │                     │
       │  SSE: data chunks     │                        │                     │
       │◀──────────────────────│                        │                     │

Safety guarantees:

  • No direct HTTP: The agent NEVER calls backend services directly — all mutations go through the MCP Gateway.
  • Schema validation: Every tool invocation is validated against a pre-registered JSON schema before execution.
  • Idempotency keys: Duplicate tool calls (from LLM retries) are deduplicated at the gateway layer.
  • Risk levels: Tools are tagged LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH. HIGH-risk tools (delete, bulk operations) require explicit user confirmation.
  • Audit trail: Every tool invocation is logged as structured JSON with caller identity, parameters, result, and latency.

5. Real-Time Analytics — Idempotent Aggregation Pipeline


ANALYTICS DATA FLOW
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

  Browser /api/track       Kafka                   Aggregator           Valkey         PostgreSQL
  ──────────────────      ─────                   ──────────          ──────         ──────────
       │                    │                         │                  │                │
       │ pageview event     │                         │                  │                │
       │ (UUIDv7 key)       │                         │                  │                │
       │───────────────────▶│                         │                  │                │
       │                    │                         │                  │                │
       │                    │  analytics.raw.events   │                  │                │
       │                    │────────────────────────▶│                  │                │
       │                    │                         │                  │                │
       │                    │              ┌──────────┴──────────┐       │                │
       │                    │              │ Batch: 100 records  │       │                │
       │                    │              │                     │       │                │
       │                    │              │ For each record:    │       │                │
       │                    │              │  1. Parse UA        │       │                │
       │                    │              │  2. HMAC-hash IP    │       │                │
       │                    │              │  3. Geo snap to     │       │                │
       │                    │              │     grid centroid   │       │                │
       │                    │              └──────────┬──────────┘       │                │
       │                    │                         │                  │                │
       │                    │                         │ SETNX dedup_key  │                │
       │                    │                         │ (24h TTL)        │                │
       │                    │                         │─────────────────▶│                │
       │                    │                         │                  │                │
       │                    │                         │ OK (new) / SKIP  │                │
       │                    │                         │◀─────────────────│                │
       │                    │                         │                  │                │
       │                    │                         │ UPSERT geo_time_rollups           │
       │                    │                         │ (5m + 1d granularity)             │
       │                    │                         │─────────────────────────────────▶│
       │                    │                         │                  │                │

Key design choices:

  • UUIDv7 event keys: Time-sortable, globally unique — enables replay without duplication.
  • HMAC-hashed IP: Privacy-preserving (no raw IPs stored), still enables unique-visitor counting.
  • Geo-snapping: GPS coordinates rounded to grid centroids — prevents fingerprinting while keeping the 3D globe visualization accurate.
  • Valkey SETNX dedup: Even if Kafka redelivers the same event (at-least-once), the SETNX gate ensures the rollup UPSERT fires at most once per event.
  • Graceful degradation: If Kafka is unreachable, /api/track falls back to a direct Supabase visitor_logs insert — the visitor is never lost.

6. Notification Service — Reliable Email Dispatch with Backoff


EMAIL DISPATCH STATE MACHINE
═════════════════════════════════════════════

                  ┌─────────┐
  Kafka event ───▶│ PENDING │
                  └────┬────┘
                       │
            Scheduler claims batch (every 15s)
                       │
                       ▼
                  ┌─────────┐
            ┌─────│ SENDING │─────┐
            │     └─────────┘     │
         success              failure
            │                     │
            ▼                     ▼
       ┌────────┐          ┌──────────┐
       │  SENT  │          │  RETRY   │ (backoff: 60s → 120s → 240s → 480s → 960s)
       └────┬───┘          └────┬─────┘
            │                   │
            │             retries > 5?
            │                   │
            │                   ▼
            │              ┌────────┐
            │              │ FAILED │
            │              └────────┘
            ▼
       ┌────────┐
       │  READ  │ (tracking pixel opened)
       └────────┘

Fan-out strategy: A single Kafka content event is expanded into N notification_recipients rows (one per subscriber with matching topic preferences). The scheduler processes them in claimed batches — preventing thundering-herd on the SMTP provider and enabling per-recipient retry isolation.

7. Technology Stack

LayerTechnologyPurpose
FrontendNext.js, TypeScript, VercelSSR, API proxy, admin dashboard, 3D globe
BackendSpring Boot 3, Java 215 services on Cloud Run
MessagingApache Kafka (Aiven)Event streaming, fan-out, DLQ
SearchOpenSearch (Aiven)BM25 + doc2query + synonym graph
DatabasePostgreSQL + pgvector (Supabase)Content, outbox, RAG embeddings
CacheValkey (Redis-compatible)Dedup, rate limiting, response cache
AI/MLGemini 2.5 Flash, GPT-4o-mini, text-embedding-3-smallIntent, chat, doc2query, RAG embeddings
InfraGoogle Cloud Run, Flyway, GitHub ActionsContainers, migrations, CI/CD
ProtocolModel Context Protocol (MCP)Typed tool gateway with schema + RBAC

8. Production Reliability Patterns

PatternWhereBenefit
Transactional OutboxAdmin ServiceNo phantom Kafka events on DB rollback
Idempotent ConsumersAll Kafka consumersSafe redelivery without duplicate side effects
Dead Letter QueueKafka DLQ topicPoison-pill isolation, manual replay
Exponential BackoffNotification dispatchGraceful SMTP recovery without thundering herd
SETNX Dedup GateAnalytics aggregatorExactly-once rollup writes
Fail-Open EnrichmentSearch Indexer (Gemini)LLM failure never blocks indexing
Graceful DegradationTrack endpointKafka down → direct DB write
Circuit Breaker (implicit)RestTemplate timeouts10s connect / 30s read prevents cascade

9. Outcome

A live system at yuqi.site demonstrating:

  • Event-driven architecture with Kafka and transactional outbox
  • Multi-model AI orchestration with RBAC and MCP tool governance
  • Cost-optimized search (BM25 + doc2query + synonyms = zero query-time LLM cost)
  • Real-time analytics with privacy-preserving aggregation
  • Reliable email delivery with state-machine dispatch
  • Five independently deployable services with zero shared mutable state

10. Live Monitoring — Grafana Cloud Observability

All eight microservices expose Prometheus metrics via Spring Boot Actuator + Micrometer. A dedicated Grafana Alloy scraper (deployed as its own Cloud Run service) collects metrics every 60 seconds and pushes them to Grafana Cloud (free tier: 10K series, 50GB logs).

Monitored Services:

  • portfolio-notification-service
  • portfolio-admin-service + search-indexer + rag-indexer
  • portfolio-agent-service + mcp-gateway
  • analytics-aggregator-service + analytics-alerts-service

Key Metrics: JVM heap/GC, HTTP request latency (p50/p95/p99), Hikari connection pool, Kafka consumer lag, thread pool utilization, error rates by endpoint.

📊 Live Public Dashboard (Grafana Cloud) · 📸 Dashboard Snapshot
